name: Sample Drum
description: Dual-channel sample player and live recorder. 16-bit/48kHz WAV playback from microSD (32MB RAM), 12-bit capture via CV3/REC on ch2. 1V/oct C0-C8, manual+auto slicing, AD envelope, virtual VCA, per-channel FX (HPF/LPF/delay/reverb/bitcrush/drive), 3 assignable CVs per channel.

controls.name: Encoder A / DATA
controls.description: Main data encoder for channel 1. Selects samples, edits values and confirms menu entries with click.
controls.detail: Click = enter/confirm · long press = sub-menu · primary navigator on ch1 page

controls.name: Encoder B
controls.description: Channel-1 parameter encoder. Page-dependent: Start, Loop Start, Attack, FX value, slice point etc.
controls.detail: Hold TRG2/SHIFT + B = secondary edit (e.g. CV scale)

controls.name: Encoder C
controls.description: Channel-1 parameter encoder. Page-dependent: End, Loop End, Decay, FX mix etc.
controls.detail: Hold TRG2/SHIFT + C for fine/secondary edit on many pages

controls.name: Encoder D / BACK
controls.description: Channel-2 data encoder. Click returns to the previous menu page from any sub-menu.
controls.detail: Click = back · long press = sub-menu · primary navigator on ch2 page

controls.name: Encoder E
controls.description: Channel-2 parameter encoder, mirrors B on channel 1.

controls.name: Encoder F
controls.description: Channel-2 parameter encoder, mirrors C on channel 1. In manual slicing mode, places slice markers when held with TRG2/SHIFT.
controls.detail: Page-dependent · slice-place in manual slice mode

controls.name: PLAY
controls.description: Manually triggers sample playback (auditioning) and is also used to start/stop recording in the record menu.
controls.detail: Audition without patching a trigger · arms/starts capture in REC mode

controls.name: TRG1
controls.description: Channel-1 manual trigger button. Doubles as channel-1 select in global menus and mirrors the TRIG 1 input.
controls.detail: Hold for menu shortcuts on some pages

controls.name: TRG2 / SHIFT
controls.description: Channel-2 manual trigger button AND the global SHIFT key. Held with encoders or other buttons it unlocks deeper menu pages, CV scale setting and manual slice placement.
controls.detail: Mirrors TRIG 2 input · Hold + A/B/C = set CV scale on those CV inputs · Hold + F (in slice menu) = drop slice marker

inputs.name: TRIG 1
inputs.description: Trigger/gate input for channel 1. Behaviour follows the channel-1 play mode (one-shot trigger, gated, looped, reversed etc.).

inputs.name: CV1 (ch 1)
inputs.description: Assignable CV input for channel 1. Routable to Tune (1V/oct), Sample Select, Slice Select, Start, End, Loop, Attack, Decay, Level, Play Mode or FX with per-input scale set by SHIFT + Encoder A.

inputs.name: CV2 (ch 1)
inputs.description: Second assignable CV input for channel 1. Same parameter routing menu and per-input scale.

inputs.name: CV3 (ch 1)
inputs.description: Third assignable CV input for channel 1. Commonly used for 1V/oct pitch when assigned to Tune.

inputs.name: CV1 (ch 2)
inputs.description: Assignable CV input for channel 2 with the same parameter menu as channel 1.

inputs.name: CV2 (ch 2)
inputs.description: Second assignable CV input for channel 2.

inputs.name: CV3 / REC (ch 2)
inputs.description: Dual-purpose. As a CV input it works like the other CVs on channel 2. In RECORD mode this jack accepts the audio signal to be sampled — set 10V range for modular signals or 1.2V for line level. Recording is 12-bit at 48kHz to RAM, then saved to the SD card.
inputs.voltage: audio in (1.2V or 10Vptp range) · or assignable CV -5V to +5V

outputs.name: OUT 1
outputs.description: Mono audio output for channel 1, post AD-envelope, virtual VCA and realtime FX.

outputs.name: OUT 2
outputs.description: Mono audio output for channel 2, post AD-envelope, virtual VCA and realtime FX.

behaviors.name: Play modes
behaviors.description: Each channel selects a play mode: Trigger (one-shot, ignores gate length), Gate (plays while gate HIGH), Loop (plays continuously while gate HIGH), Reverse (one-shot backwards) and other firmware-added modes. Mode is per channel and CV-assignable.

behaviors.name: 1V/oct pitch tracking
behaviors.description: Assign any CV input to Tune for chromatic playback across C0-C8. Higher pitches shorten the sample (no time-stretch); the module becomes a melodic sample voice.

behaviors.name: AD envelope + virtual VCA
behaviors.description: Each channel has an Attack-Decay envelope feeding a virtual VCA. Attack and Decay are edited on a dedicated page and can be CV-assigned for per-hit dynamics.

behaviors.name: Realtime FX bay
behaviors.description: Per-channel realtime effect chosen from HPF, LPF, delay, reverb, bitcrusher and drive. One effect active at a time; parameter and mix are CV-assignable.

behaviors.name: Sample recording (CV3/REC ch2)
behaviors.description: Records audio patched to the CV3/REC jack on channel 2 to RAM at 48kHz/12-bit. Set 10V range for modular signals, 1.2V for line level. Captured material is immediately playable on either channel and can be saved to SD.

behaviors.name: Manual + automatic slicing
behaviors.description: Slices can be placed automatically (linear or zero-crossing detection across the sample) or by hand (TRG2/SHIFT + Encoder F drops a marker at the playhead). Cue points stored in WAV files load as slices. From firmware 1.6 a CV assigned to Slice Select chooses slices via 1V/oct.

behaviors.name: CV input assignment & scale
behaviors.description: Each of the three CV inputs per channel routes to one parameter (Tune, Sample Select, Slice Select, Start, End, Loop, Attack, Decay, Level, Play Mode, FX). Per-input scale/depth is set by holding TRG2/SHIFT and turning Encoder A/B/C while watching the on-screen scope.

behaviors.name: Preset save/load
behaviors.description: Save complete module state (sample assignments, CV routings, FX, envelopes) as a preset on the SD card and recall it during performance for instant kit changes.

behaviors.name: No internal modulation
behaviors.description: Sample Drum has no internal LFO, S&H or envelope generator beyond the AD per channel. All modulation (slow morphs, sample-select randomness etc.) must come from external modules patched into the CV inputs.

patchIdeas.name: Dual one-shot drum voice
patchIdeas.patch: Clock sequencer triggers the two channels independently for a stereo drum pair.
steps: Load a kick on channel 1 and a snare/clap on channel 2 (rotate Encoder A / D over a sample list).
Set Ch1 Play Mode = Trigger, Ch2 Play Mode = Trigger (one-shots, ignore gate length).
Set Attack ~0 and Decay to taste on both channels (envelope page).
Patch trigger sequencer track 1 -> TRIG 1, track 2 -> TRIG 2.
Patch OUT 1 -> mixer L, OUT 2 -> mixer R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 / OUT 2 - two independent mono drum hits at ~10Vptp.
patchIdeas.hear: A clean two-voice drum kit firing in tight time. Tweak Decay for shorter/longer tails; swap samples on Encoder A/D for instant kit changes.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAMPLE DRUM:
* Ch1 Mode: Trigger
* Ch2 Mode: Trigger
- Seq (Trk1) t> Sample Drum (TRIG 1)
- Seq (Trk2) t> Sample Drum (TRIG 2)
- Sample Drum (OUT 1) -> Mixer (L)
- Sample Drum (OUT 2) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Melodic chromatic sample (1V/oct)
patchIdeas.patch: Quantizer drives Tune for chromatic melodies; sequencer fires hits.
steps: Load a tonal one-shot (mallet, vocal vowel, plucked string) on channel 1.
Open the CV menu on ch1 and assign CV3 -> Tune. Hold TRG2/SHIFT + Encoder C and set the scale to 1V/oct (5 octaves cover C0-C8).
Set Ch1 Play Mode = Trigger, short Attack, medium Decay.
Patch quantizer CV out -> CV3 (ch1).
Patch sequencer gate -> TRIG 1.
Patch OUT 1 -> VCF or mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 - the loaded sample retriggered chromatically across the quantizer's range.
patchIdeas.hear: A monophonic melodic sample voice. Higher notes shorten the sample (no time-stretch), so very high pitches become percussive blips - keep notes within ~3 octaves of the original pitch for natural results.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAMPLE DRUM:
* Ch1 Mode: Trigger
* Ch1 CV3: Tune (1V/oct)
- Quant (CV) p> Sample Drum (CV3 ch1)
- Seq (Gate) g> Sample Drum (TRIG 1)
- Sample Drum (OUT 1) -> VCF (In)

patchIdeas.name: Loop with moving start point
patchIdeas.patch: Long sample looped while an external LFO walks the start point for shifting textures.
steps: Load a long sample (pad, field recording, drone) on channel 1.
Set Ch1 Play Mode = Loop.
Open the CV menu on ch1 and assign CV1 -> Start. Hold TRG2/SHIFT + Encoder A and set a moderate scale.
Patch a slow LFO (e.g. 0.05Hz triangle) -> CV1 (ch1).
Patch a sustained gate -> TRIG 1 (HIGH = loop running).
Patch OUT 1 -> reverb -> mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 - the same loop running continuously, but its start point drifts with the LFO.
patchIdeas.hear: A morphing texture - the sample loops at its full length, but each pass enters at a different point so the timbre slowly shifts. Add a second LFO into CV2 (assigned to End) for two-axis movement.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAMPLE DRUM:
* Ch1 Mode: Loop
* Ch1 CV1: Start
- Gate (Hi) g> Sample Drum (TRIG 1)
- LFO1 (Out) >> Sample Drum (CV1 ch1)
- Sample Drum (OUT 1) -> Reverb (In)

patchIdeas.name: Reverse hit with envelope-controlled tail
patchIdeas.patch: Reverse playback with an external envelope shortening/lengthening the Decay per hit.
steps: Load a cymbal or vocal sample on channel 1.
Set Ch1 Play Mode = Reverse.
Open the CV menu on ch1 and assign CV1 -> Decay. Hold TRG2/SHIFT + Encoder A to set the depth.
Patch the same trigger to TRIG 1 AND to an external envelope generator's Trig.
Patch the external envelope's CV out -> CV1 (ch1).
Patch OUT 1 -> mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 - the sample played backwards with a per-hit dynamic decay.
patchIdeas.hear: Reverse-cymbal swells whose tail length follows the envelope. Lengthen the envelope's decay for slow swells; shorten it for tight reverse blips. Sample Drum has no internal LFO/EG, so this requires an external envelope.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAMPLE DRUM:
* Ch1 Mode: Reverse
* Ch1 CV1: Decay
- Trig (Out) t> Sample Drum (TRIG 1)
- Trig (Out) t> Env (Trig)
- Env (Out) >> Sample Drum (CV1 ch1)
- Sample Drum (OUT 1) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Live loop sliced and rearranged
patchIdeas.patch: Record a 1-bar loop into the module, auto-slice it, then random-CV step through the slices.
steps: Patch a drum loop or beat into CV3/REC (ch2). Set the input range: 10V for modular, 1.2V for line.
Hold TRG2/SHIFT and enter the RECORD menu. Press PLAY to capture one bar, press PLAY again to stop.
Open the slice menu and run Auto-Slice (zero-crossing) - the loop is chopped into slices.
Assign CV1 (ch1) -> Slice Select (firmware 1.6+). Set scale to 1V/oct or to the number of slices.
Patch a random/stepped CV (e.g. Turing Machine) -> CV1 (ch1).
Patch your master clock -> TRIG 1.
Patch OUT 1 -> mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 - one slice fired per clock pulse, slice index chosen by the random CV.
patchIdeas.hear: A glitched, rearranged version of your original loop. Quantize the random CV for tonal slicing; freeze it for repeating slice loops.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAMPLE DRUM:
* Ch1 Mode: Trigger
* Ch1 CV1: Slice Select (1V/oct)
- Source (Audio) -> Sample Drum (CV3/REC ch2)
- Clock (Out) c> Sample Drum (TRIG 1)
- Random (CV) >> Sample Drum (CV1 ch1)
- Sample Drum (OUT 1) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo dual-sample pair
patchIdeas.patch: Same trigger fires both channels with different samples for a hard-stereo composite hit.
steps: Load complementary samples on each channel (e.g. transient on ch1, body on ch2).
Set both Play Modes = Trigger.
Use Encoder B / E to set Start a few ms apart for thickness.
Mult one trigger to TRIG 1 AND TRIG 2.
Patch OUT 1 -> mixer L, OUT 2 -> mixer R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 (L) + OUT 2 (R) - hard-panned stereo composite sample, ~10Vptp each.
patchIdeas.hear: A wider, layered drum or vocal hit. Detune one channel slightly with SHIFT + Encoder B (Tune offset) for chorus-like width.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAMPLE DRUM:
* Ch1 Mode: Trigger
* Ch2 Mode: Trigger
- Trig (Out) t> Sample Drum (TRIG 1)
- Trig (Out) t> Sample Drum (TRIG 2)
- Sample Drum (OUT 1) -> Mixer (L)
- Sample Drum (OUT 2) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: CV-swept sample bank
patchIdeas.patch: A stepped CV walks through a folder of samples for ever-changing one-shots.
steps: Load a folder of samples from SD onto channel 1 (rotate Encoder A through the list to confirm they're loaded in RAM).
Set Ch1 Play Mode = Trigger.
Assign CV1 (ch1) -> Sample Select. Hold TRG2/SHIFT + Encoder A and set the scale so the full CV range covers all loaded samples.
Patch a stepped CV (sequencer or Turing Machine) -> CV1 (ch1).
Patch the same step's gate -> TRIG 1.
Patch OUT 1 -> mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 - a different sample fired per step.
patchIdeas.hear: Each step plays a new sample from your folder. Great for evolving percussion lines. From firmware 1.6 the sample-select CV tracks 1V/oct, so a quantized melody can also pick samples chromatically.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAMPLE DRUM:
* Ch1 Mode: Trigger
* Ch1 CV1: Sample Select
- Seq (CV) >> Sample Drum (CV1 ch1)
- Seq (Gate) g> Sample Drum (TRIG 1)
- Sample Drum (OUT 1) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Bitcrushed drum bus
patchIdeas.patch: Use the per-channel realtime FX (bitcrusher + drive) to lo-fi a drum hit and modulate the FX amount with CV.
steps: Load a drum sample on channel 1, Play Mode = Trigger.
On the FX page select Bitcrusher (or Drive). Set a moderate amount with Encoder C.
Open the CV menu and assign CV2 (ch1) -> FX. Hold TRG2/SHIFT + Encoder B to set the depth.
Patch a slow LFO or envelope follower -> CV2 (ch1).
Patch your trigger -> TRIG 1.
Patch OUT 1 -> mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 - the drum hit with bitcrushed/driven character that breathes with the modulation CV.
patchIdeas.hear: A drum that's clean on quiet hits and crunchy on loud ones (with an envelope follower), or an FX amount that drifts with an LFO. Switch between LPF/HPF/Delay/Reverb on the FX page for radically different colours from the same sample.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAMPLE DRUM:
* Ch1 Mode: Trigger
* Ch1 FX: Bitcrusher
* Ch1 CV2: FX
- Trig (Out) t> Sample Drum (TRIG 1)
- LFO (Out) >> Sample Drum (CV2 ch1)
- Sample Drum (OUT 1) -> Mixer (In)
